description: CymR-repressible synthetic promoter: a sigma70 core (-35/-10) flanked by two cumate operators (CuO); CymR binding represses transcription and the inducer p-cumate relieves it

references.authors: Meyer AJ, Segall-Shapiro TH, Glassey E, Zhang J, Voigt CA
references.title: Escherichia coli "Marionette" strains with 12 highly optimized small-molecule sensors
p-Cymene catabolic pathway in Pseudomonas putida F1: cloning and characterization of DNA encoding conversion of p-cymene to p-cumate

label: PCymRC
note: CymR-repressible synthetic promoter: a sigma70 core (-35/-10) flanked by two cumate operators (CuO); CymR binding represses transcription and the inducer p-cumate relieves it

label: CuO_1
note: Cumate operator (CuO / CymO): 32-bp CymR-binding site (per the MH101728 annotation); overlaps the -35 so CymR binding occludes RNA polymerase
-35 box (TTGTAT; conserved TTG), matching the minus_35_signal annotated in GenBank MH101728; overlaps the 3' end of CuO_1, 17-bp spacer to the -10
-10 box (TATAAT, consensus), matching the minus_10_signal annotated in GenBank MH101728; 17-bp spacer from the -35
Transcription start site (per the MH101728 annotation); coincides with the 5' end of CuO_2

label: CuO_2
note: Cumate operator (CuO / CymO): second 32-bp CymR-binding site (per the MH101728 annotation), spanning the +1 transcription start

provenance.sequence_source: PCymRC promoter (90 bp), as annotated in GenBank MH101728 (cloning vector pAJM.657; Meyer, Segall-Shapiro, Voigt 2018), bases 73-162. The identical 90-bp sequence is also present in Addgene pAJM.712 (#108513, PCymRC-YFP), fetched via tools/addgene.py. The sequence and the -35/-10/operator/+1 coordinates are byte- and coordinate-exact to the MH101728 annotation.
provenance.annotation_note: Boundaries, the two CuO/CymO operators, the -35, the -10 and the +1 follow the author annotation in GenBank MH101728; the 90-bp sequence was cross-verified byte-for-byte against Addgene pAJM.712 (#108513).

functional_claims.label: Repression by CymR is relieved by the inducer p-cumate (p-isopropylbenzoate): CymR binds the CuO operators to repress, and p-cumate de-represses.

source.quote: cymR encodes a repressor which controls expression of both the cym and cmt operons and is inducible by p-cumate but not p-cymene

functional_claims.label: Characterized in Escherichia coli (the sensor was integrated into the E. coli MG1655, DH10B and BL21 genomes).

source.quote: the genomes of E. coli MG1655 (wild-type), DH10B (cloning), and BL21 (protein expression)

functional_claims.label: PCymRC is one of a 12-sensor set engineered for >100-fold induction with low background; a PCymRC-specific value is not separated in the source abstract.

value.scope: Marionette sensor panel (12 sensors incl. PCymRC), not PCymRC-specific

source.quote: a set of 12 high-performance sensors that exhibit >100-fold induction with low background and cross-reactivity
